Comment:
Haven't been to a McDonald's in a hot minute, but I had an 'itch' for something cold and greasy. Well, itch scratched! The filet o' fish has often been a safe bet, but not this time. First of all, I don't remember them being the size of Crystal sliders, especially a double, but not only are the boxes smaller, the 'sandwich' was nearly lost in there - had it not been for the ton of (requested) 'extra' tartar sauce that was EVERYWHERE - except in the sandwich. I guess that was in an attempt to disguise the fact that the 'filets' had quite obviously been fried, and then kept warm-ish since the lunch rush (5 hours prior). Actually, they were so dark brown and crunchy, they might have been refried...possibly more than once. And then my other guilty pleasure, reaching into the bag on the seat next to me to start eating a few French fries on the drive home, was also ruined. As i pulled back out onto the street and reached into the large red cup, I could not get any fries. At a stop light, upon pulling the container out, I found that my $3,99 large fries had only been filled about 2/3 of the way...fully an inch-plus below the top and what was included in that reduced mass was a pile of soggy, warm-ish curled up, salt-less fries, also probably left over from the lunch rush - hours ago. So, I spent $10.79 (without a drink) for 'food' that went into the trash as soon as I pulled into the garage.
Do better Ronald, not that it matters; it will be a LONG time before, even if I ever do, waste any of my money there again.
name: dAVID mACfARLANE

Comment:
Poor Customer Service Experience – 801 Hwy 81 E McDonough GA– 12 AUG 2025 (9:35-9:40 AM)

This morning, I visited your Lake Dow Location around 9:35-9:40AM to order your Mix & Match breakfast deal (two breakfast sandwiches for $4.79, as advertised on your drive-thru signage).

When I asked about the deal, the first employee told me they were “doing breakfast,” which I already knew. I clarified that I wanted the advertised 2-for-$4.79 deal. Another employee joined the conversation and stated there were “two different deals.” Both employees spoke to me in a tone that came across as condescending, as if I didn’t understand the menu — which was both disrespectful and unnecessary.

When I began to list the first sandwich I wanted, I was interrupted before I could finish. I explained that I was about to give the second one, and the employee told me it wasn’t part of the deal — even though it was clearly displayed on the signage at the menu board.

This interaction was frustrating for two reasons:
1.	The tone and attitude from both employees were unprofessional and made me feel demeaned as a customer.
2.	Staff should be aware of the promotions displayed at their own location to avoid giving customers incorrect information.

I left without ordering because I could feel myself becoming upset, and I didn’t want to escalate the situation. However, I believe this store would greatly benefit from additional training on customer service etiquette and product knowledge.

Please look into this matter and address it with the employees who were working the drive-thru at approximately 9:35- 9:40 AM.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sy’nae Johns
name: Sy'nae Johns

You can check by the look of the cheese and texture of the bite of the bagel. A tough bite means that bagel was reused from an old bagel. The bite should be to break apart with your bite. If I have to work, I buy their breakfast, but I have their app to gain tons of points so I can use them one day. The money may be funny, or I don't feel like using my card. There are no cards for 2025. This is not a resolution it is just a solution to a  problem from 2024(Prime2025). So people, please give McDonald's a chance and try the steak bagel. McDonald's, please make sure it's fresh. No one likes refurbished food, let alone room temp food. Thank you, McDonald's.

P.S. French Fries, no salt yummy!!!!! Fresh fries, not double dip fries in the grease after sitting first awhile.

Comment: Was literally just verbally harassed by an employee. Going through the drive through around time that school lets out so it was pretty busy. I jump in, like everyone else, and wait about 10 minutes to make it to the speaker to order. The lady asks me what she can get for me and before I could even say a word she cuts me off to say their speakers are broken and I need to order inside. “Your speakers are broken but I can hear you perfectly fine?” I asked, to which she RESPONDS, “Yes ma’am we’re having trouble hearing anything I can see you talking but I can’t hear you.” “But you’re responding to me?” I say “Yes but they’re going out I can’t hear you?” she responds. “But you’re literally responding to me how could they be broken” I say. Then silence. For about 5 minutes while they proceeded to take orders from the other lane. I sat there. A few minutes later I hear “Ma’am you’re holding up my line it would probably be good cardio to get some steps in and come inside.” At this point I’m pissed because how dare you comment on my weight when she looked like she eats big macs FOR A LIVING and I am literally perfectly healthy. Yes your line is being held up because YOU WONT TAKE MY ORDER.  Regardless it’s so unprofessional and immature to say to a customer. So I respond back “Baby I’m a preschool teacher AND I have a second job I get PLENTY of steps. I’m not the one that’s probably overweight” Silence for another 5 minutes. Then someone magically comes over the “broken” speaker and takes my order! Wow I guess they weren’t broken to begin with. She just didn’t like that I could tell I was being lied to. She just didn’t like the fact that I didn’t keep driving. She simply did not want to take my order and to be so incredibly rude to a customer like that, make comments about their weight, and literally try to bully them out of the drive through is INSANE. I work fast food as well and I could never imagine talking to a customer this way. She needs to be fired or seriously talked to, and y’all need to get some employees that actually care about their customers and have more than 3 brain cells and yes I did talk to a manager but these reviews seem to be the thing y’all’s supervisors care about! I hope this location has a horrible day.

Comment:
Delays. Errors. Repetition. They're doing their best I'm sure, it's just if I can drive to the other locations nearby... I usually do. If I have to go to this one because I'm too tired to drive further up the road, I try to have extra patience. They are doing their best but it's rare that an order is handed over without some additional needs or basics missing. I don't know who would even be responsible for that.
Specifically, issues have been with special orders, regular orders, missing drinks, wrong drink, you say okay to cheese but the patty is old and bread stale, you say no cheese and they put cheese anyways. It's kind of a game at this point to see whether some of the order is even up to an inspection standard. Usually it's not. The people are nice, they're probably tired tho. That might contribute to the mistakes. Perhaps low morale as well, for that I think incentives for a tired workforce would probably go a looooong way to helping the customers have a more consistently better experience.
Again, I don't mind the wait when I anticipate it's going to be a long one. Sometimes tho it would be nice to get fast, decent food.

Was actually going to charge the full amount of a large beverage. And when I stressed this has never happened before the employee at the window stated that it depends on which manager is on duty. So now I’m confused. Was the rule changed and patrons are required to pay beverage prices for a large cup of ice? Or does the Manager on Duty at the time an order is placed make the decision as to whether or not someone gets charged for a cup of ice? If so that doesn’t seem like fair business practices. Inconsiderate & Self-centered management practices. Went thru the drive-through on 11/22/24 at 7:30 am. Ordered two breakfast sandwiches and hash browns. I asked the manager why a customer should suffer when it was McDonald's mistake? The manager's ridiculous excuse was that she had no cash register at this window. This was not the 1st time I've experienced the same issue at this McDonald's. So, I've had similar experiences at other McDonald's locations and have never been told to go get back into the drive-through line. In fact, for such a small item, they would just give it to me. I do not recommend patronizing this McDonald's location.
